failure_modes.text: The need to be right can overpower the search for truth and block disconfirming evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Define what evidence would disprove the hypothesis before seeking confirmation; the need to be right is the enemy of the search for truth.

failure_modes.text: A team can torture the facts until they support the favored hypothesis instead of treating the hypothesis as provisional.
failure_modes.mitigation: Treat the hypothesis as provisional: if the facts have to be tortured to fit, the hypothesis should change, not the facts.

failure_modes.text: Groupthink or systemic inertia can stop the team from challenging the leading belief even when the evidence is turning against it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Create explicit challenge mechanisms that override groupthink and systemic inertia when the evidence is turning against the leading belief.

premortem_questions.text: What single fact, if true, would cause us to abandon this hypothesis immediately?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What single fact, if true, would cause us to abandon this hypothesis immediately?

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: By how much would your assumptions need to change for your conclusion to be wrong?
premortem_questions.source_quote: By how much would your assumptions need to change... for your conclusion to be wrong?

heuristics.text: Use strong, testable hypotheses because they are easier to challenge and pressure test than vague claims.
Institutionalize a dialectic standard so every thesis meets an antithesis before synthesis.
Stress-test the assumptions underneath the recommendation rather than only the recommendation itself.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on reversal conditions and hypothesis challenge, so the chunk set stays close to those core moves.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor philosophical commentary that widened beyond operational test design.
open_questions: The third premortem question is lightly normalized from the raw wording so it stays compact as a chunk.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on intellectual humility and first principles for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is rich on confirmation bias, commitment, motivated reasoning, and hammer effects.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against confirmation-bias and commitment-and-consistency-bias.
